f7dd7ca7-fd92-4272-a2f3-0d71fbd967fa.name: Supplier Questionnaire
f7dd7ca7-fd92-4272-a2f3-0d71fbd967fa.lifecycle: Completed by prospective supplier; retained as part of qualification file.

3dad5836-8dd0-4732-a84a-5efa09c84ba1.name: Structured COA Data Schema
3dad5836-8dd0-4732-a84a-5efa09c84ba1.format: data schema (per-lot record with a repeating parameter list)
3dad5836-8dd0-4732-a84a-5efa09c84ba1.lifecycle: Defines how a Certificate of Analysis's contents are captured as structured data instead of read manually off a PDF. Header fields (once per COA): supplier id, material/product id, lot number, COA issue date, received-lot reference. Repeating parameter list (one row per tested parameter, mirroring what the Material / Product Specification defines for that material): parameter name (e.g. moisture, aflatoxin, aerobic plate count), reported value, unit, spec limit type (min / max / range / target), spec limit value(s), and a derived pass/fail flag. The pass/fail flag is computed automatically by comparing reported value against the matching parameter on the Material / Product Specification — this comparison, done today by a person reading a PDF against a paper spec sheet, is the specific manual step Module 4 flags as the automation target. A COA with any parameter flagged fail routes to disposition (reject lot / use-as-is deviation / return to supplier) rather than being released to inventory automatically.

03dcbbf1-8518-4030-bb66-c10e4c777a5f.name: Supplier Risk Assessment Record
03dcbbf1-8518-4030-bb66-c10e4c777a5f.lifecycle: Created during qualification; reviewed periodically to adjust verification intensity. The tier assigned here is looked up from the Supplier Risk Scoring Matrix (see that artifact).

d3ffd93c-4dc3-4497-a82e-158181ac79e3.name: Supplier Risk Scoring Matrix
d3ffd93c-4dc3-4497-a82e-158181ac79e3.lifecycle: Maintained by food safety/quality leadership; revisited on major recall events or annual program review. Combines two inputs into a Risk Tier that sets required verification intensity: Ingredient Criticality (High — e.g. RTE, major allergen, prior recall history; Medium; Low — e.g. shelf-stable, no recall history) crossed with Supplier Performance History (Good/Fair/Poor, per the Supplier Performance Scorecard). The resulting tier drives audit frequency and testing/COA frequency: High tier → audit every 6–12 months, COA plus testing every lot; Medium tier → audit every 12–24 months, COA every lot, periodic testing; Low tier → audit every 24–36 months, COA every lot, testing via receipt sampling plan. These specific frequency bands are this design's own risk-based verification policy, not a citation to a codified FSMA/GFSI numeric schedule — FSMA Preventive Controls (21 CFR 117.410/117.425) requires an onsite audit before first use and at least annually for suppliers controlling a serious hazard, with no fixed tiered schedule of its own, and GFSI schemes similarly default to annual audits absent a documented risk-based justification for reduced frequency. A supplier's tier can only improve or worsen at the next scheduled review — a single new data point (e.g. one failed audit) escalates it immediately.

4e16ee4f-d132-44e5-ac05-b88b4767e5ff.name: GFSI-Recognized Audit Report
4e16ee4f-d132-44e5-ac05-b88b4767e5ff.lifecycle: Issued by third-party auditor (SQF/BRC/FSSC); collected at qualification and on a recurring annual surveillance-audit cadence, with full re-certification typically every 3 years for schemes like FSSC 22000/BRCGS.

5ecdd375-c8ce-4b2f-a069-1d1948c844e2.name: Supplier Certification Record
5ecdd375-c8ce-4b2f-a069-1d1948c844e2.lifecycle: Collected at qualification for organic/kosher/halal/gluten-free/etc. certifications; renewed on certificate expiry.

56526379-12e6-4c0f-a1fc-bd1c83d9f1a0.name: Material / Product Specification
56526379-12e6-4c0f-a1fc-bd1c83d9f1a0.lifecycle: Authored at supplier or product qualification; revised under change control; referenced by receiving, in-process QC, and label copy. Each specified parameter (name, unit, limit type, limit value) is what a Structured COA Data Schema record is checked against (see that artifact).

4c7a9c60-4652-4ccd-8a99-de688943fdf3.name: Lab Test Result
4c7a9c60-4652-4ccd-8a99-de688943fdf3.lifecycle: Generated per test event by internal or external lab; compared against specification limits.

e633d7e7-fd2f-458b-b7e0-837e92dbb64a.name: Approved Supplier List (ASL)
e633d7e7-fd2f-458b-b7e0-837e92dbb64a.lifecycle: Updated on supplier approval, suspension, or removal; referenced by procurement before any PO is placed.

69f4ab73-992a-4852-b94e-0440b2304521.name: Certificate of Analysis (COA)
69f4ab73-992a-4852-b94e-0440b2304521.lifecycle: Issued by supplier per shipped lot; reviewed at receiving and retained for the lot's traceability record. Structurally, its contents follow the Structured COA Data Schema (see that artifact) — as-received it's still typically a PDF, but the fields it contains map 1:1 onto that schema for automated comparison.

234121ca-a7cb-4c99-8a09-7e947e62d724.name: Supplier Performance Scorecard
234121ca-a7cb-4c99-8a09-7e947e62d724.format: recurring report, one per supplier, typically quarterly
234121ca-a7cb-4c99-8a09-7e947e62d724.lifecycle: Generated on a recurring cadence (typically quarterly) by rolling up three tracked metrics since the prior scorecard: on-time delivery rate (% of shipments received within the agreed window), COA exception rate (% of received lots whose COA required follow-up — out-of-spec result, missing required field, late submission), and audit/re-audit trend (pass/minor-findings/major-findings across the supplier's audit history). Each metric is rated Good/Fair/Poor against a documented threshold (e.g. on-time ≥95% = Good, 85–95% = Fair, <85% = Poor) — a typical industry KPI convention, not tied to a specific regulation; the three ratings combine into the overall Supplier Performance History rating referenced by the Supplier Risk Scoring Matrix, which is what actually determines required audit and testing frequency going forward. A Poor rating on any single metric triggers an off-cycle review rather than waiting for the next scheduled scorecard.

fc2cc2d7-c0e6-4a2c-b0e8-d110914627d2.name: Advance Ship Notice (ASN / EDI 856)
fc2cc2d7-c0e6-4a2c-b0e8-d110914627d2.lifecycle: Sent by shipper before truck arrival; consumed by receiver's WMS to pre-stage the receipt.

3877cc5e-51b7-411c-8dd0-f3cbfd93306d.name: Receiving Log
3877cc5e-51b7-411c-8dd0-f3cbfd93306d.lifecycle: Created at dock receipt; one entry per inbound shipment.

620aaecb-0b8f-4336-8f3e-86716178923b.name: Temperature Record
620aaecb-0b8f-4336-8f3e-86716178923b.lifecycle: Captured continuously or at checkpoints (receiving, thawing, transport); retained for cold-chain and regulatory evidence.

49339f48-e8be-4bdb-a9d9-58a6193d1ac9.name: Receiving Inspection Record
49339f48-e8be-4bdb-a9d9-58a6193d1ac9.lifecycle: Created per lot at incoming inspection; references the material specification and drives disposition.

9019fee0-1775-4638-a520-fece292e426e.name: Hold / Quarantine Tag
9019fee0-1775-4638-a520-fece292e426e.format: physical tag and/or system status flag
9019fee0-1775-4638-a520-fece292e426e.lifecycle: Applied when material fails inspection or awaits testing; removed only on formal release.

733c42da-63cb-4309-b90f-eada2c4a97f7.name: Inventory Location Record
733c42da-63cb-4309-b90f-eada2c4a97f7.lifecycle: Updated on every putaway, pick, and move; core input to FEFO (First-Expired-First-Out) logic and mock recall inventory tracing.

e22bc4a2-d974-46c6-ac7b-9c3c1b5b3e04.name: Allergen Matrix
e22bc4a2-d974-46c6-ac7b-9c3c1b5b3e04.lifecycle: Maintained by quality/formulation; updated whenever a product formulation or production line assignment changes; consumed by scheduling and label review. Each product's row is the input set the Allergen Sequencing Algorithm reads to determine run order.

29c66c1b-1d4b-4c1a-92b4-2c951493253d.name: Allergen Sequencing Algorithm
29c66c1b-1d4b-4c1a-92b4-2c951493253d.lifecycle: Consumed by the production scheduling system whenever the day/week's run list is ordered for a line. Formalizes the conceptual "fewer to more allergens" principle (a widely-taught production-scheduling heuristic in food safety practice) into an original subset-ordering rule, not itself a named published methodology: represent each candidate run as its allergen set from the Allergen Matrix; a run A may be sequenced immediately before run B without a full allergen changeover only if A's allergen set is a subset of B's (nothing in A is absent from B, so no allergen is being removed from the line). Within that constraint, order runs so allergen-free products go first, then order the rest by increasing set size (fewest allergens to most), breaking ties by shelf-life/due-date priority. Any pair of adjacent runs where B's set does NOT contain all of A's allergens requires a full changeover — the algorithm's output includes an explicit changeover flag on the transition into each run so the schedule captures required cleaning time (see Allergen Changeover Cleaning Record) rather than treating it as free capacity. When no valid subset ordering can clear the full run list without changeovers (unavoidable with certain allergen-set combinations), the algorithm minimizes the total count of changeovers rather than eliminating them, since full elimination isn't always possible.

88e93798-54de-421a-9d7c-cc54f65d4a47.name: Demand Forecast
88e93798-54de-421a-9d7c-cc54f65d4a47.lifecycle: Regenerated on a rolling basis (weekly/monthly) over a 4-13 week horizon; feeds the MPS.

429b1edf-4726-4530-b8db-680ec0761622.name: Bill of Materials (BOM) / Recipe
429b1edf-4726-4530-b8db-680ec0761622.lifecycle: Created at formulation, revised under change control whenever the recipe changes; the FD&C Act's misbranding provisions require the label's ingredient statement to accurately reflect the actual formulation on a continuous basis, so the BOM and the label ingredient statement must stay in sync as an ongoing obligation, not a one-time check.

b6417e04-03ad-400f-bbe3-ba9a111eaeb9.name: Master Production Schedule (MPS)
b6417e04-03ad-400f-bbe3-ba9a111eaeb9.lifecycle: Rebuilt on a rolling cadence from demand forecast and capacity constraints; drives MRP and line scheduling.

3250a117-60b4-4053-b8cf-bf13f59faa1b.name: Purchase Order (EDI 850)
3250a117-60b4-4053-b8cf-bf13f59faa1b.lifecycle: Generated by MRP or procurement; acknowledged by supplier (EDI 855); closed on receipt.

6eabd20b-f5d1-4efa-9f14-e80efb204c97.name: Production / Work Order
6eabd20b-f5d1-4efa-9f14-e80efb204c97.lifecycle: Released from the MPS to the floor; drives material staging and batch record creation.

29b066a1-910f-45b5-bd65-205d53da0ba4.name: Batch / Production Record
29b066a1-910f-45b5-bd65-205d53da0ba4.lifecycle: Opened at batching, populated through the run (ingredients, parameters, CCP results), closed and retained at run completion — the core traceability record for a lot.

83d48320-9598-4069-b091-c032b4e36fd7.name: Line Clearance Record
83d48320-9598-4069-b091-c032b4e36fd7.lifecycle: Completed before each run start, especially after an allergen changeover; verifies the line is clear before production resumes.

7a252b54-ea94-4cd4-ac17-c4e00c174340.name: HACCP Plan
7a252b54-ea94-4cd4-ac17-c4e00c174340.lifecycle: Authored by the HACCP team; reviewed/revalidated at least annually or on process/hazard change per USDA FSIS meat/poultry HACCP requirements (9 CFR 417.4(a)(3)(i)) — FDA-regulated facilities under Preventive Controls for Human Food (21 CFR 117.170) instead require reanalysis at least every 3 years plus triggered reanalysis on significant change, a different cadence. Governs CCP monitoring. Contains one CCP Definition (see that artifact) per CCP in scope.

e8d03bfb-7a45-46f4-80ee-be037dd5e218.name: CCP Definition
e8d03bfb-7a45-46f4-80ee-be037dd5e218.format: structured record, one per CCP, held within the HACCP Plan
e8d03bfb-7a45-46f4-80ee-be037dd5e218.lifecycle: Authored/revised whenever the HACCP Plan is authored or revalidated. Captures, per CCP: its critical limit(s) (e.g. cook temp ≥ 165°F, a real and widely-used FSIS critical-limit example for poultry cooking to achieve Salmonella lethality), required monitoring frequency, and a reference to its corrective-action procedure. Every CCP Monitoring Log entry references exactly one CCP Definition and is checked against that definition's critical limit; every Corrective Action Record traces back to the CCP Definition that was exceeded via the monitoring event that failed.

1bdfade9-acc5-4535-891f-87d4cf762f5c.name: CCP Monitoring Log
1bdfade9-acc5-4535-891f-87d4cf762f5c.lifecycle: Generated per monitoring event at the required frequency; the primary evidence a CCP was under control. Each entry references the CCP Definition it monitors and carries the observed value plus pass/fail against that definition's critical limit; a failing entry is what triggers a Corrective Action Record.

1123026c-158f-41ad-acb2-9f67b6fb4e09.name: Corrective Action Record
1123026c-158f-41ad-acb2-9f67b6fb4e09.lifecycle: Opened on a CCP deviation or nonconformance; tracked to closure with root cause and verification of effectiveness. When opened for a CCP deviation, references the specific failing CCP Monitoring Log entry and, through it, the CCP Definition whose critical limit was exceeded.

238f08d4-2e35-498a-817d-683f36822b65.name: Allergen Changeover Cleaning Record
238f08d4-2e35-498a-817d-683f36822b65.lifecycle: Created at each allergen changeover; includes cleaning verification (visual/ELISA/ATP) results before the line can resume.

97409332-015e-4473-a32b-26b71bc67d07.name: Master Sanitation Schedule (MSS)
97409332-015e-4473-a32b-26b71bc67d07.lifecycle: Maintained as a standing prerequisite-program document; each entry executed and verified per its defined frequency.

4f930f5c-736e-451e-bd14-7a2b0e9538b1.name: Environmental Monitoring Record
4f930f5c-736e-451e-bd14-7a2b0e9538b1.lifecycle: Created per swab event; trended over time to detect pathogen harborage patterns, especially Listeria in RTE facilities. Each record is tagged with its site's EMP zone (see EMP Zone & Trending Standard) — trending is done per site, grouped by zone, not as one undifferentiated pool of results.

28e2958f-d4ed-42e4-8c0f-0de1c418f5e1.name: EMP Zone & Trending Standard
28e2958f-d4ed-42e4-8c0f-0de1c418f5e1.lifecycle: Maintained by food safety leadership; revised when the site map changes (new equipment, layout change) or after a positive finding prompts adding sites. Every swab site is assigned one of 4 zones by proximity to exposed product: Zone 1 (food-contact surfaces), Zone 2 (non-food-contact surfaces near product, e.g. equipment housings), Zone 3 (surfaces further from product but within the room, e.g. floors/drains/walls), Zone 4 (outside the immediate production area, e.g. hallways, break rooms) — a 4-zone model and "search and destroy" terminology consistent with FDA draft guidance on Listeria control in RTE foods and widespread industry practice. Required swab frequency and acceptable-positive-rate tighten by zone (Zone 1 has the lowest tolerance and highest frequency). Each site is trended individually over its rolling swab history; this design's escalation trigger (two consecutive positives at the same Zone 2/3 site, or any Zone 1 positive, escalates to intensified swabbing of that site plus adjacent sites, root-cause investigation, and a hold until 3 consecutive clean results confirm the harborage is eliminated) is common industry practice, not an FDA-codified numeric threshold — FDA's own guidance emphasizes root-cause investigation over a rigid escalation count and does not mandate these specific numbers.

19a94ed7-40fa-43b5-9868-a2ce2e2b1cef.name: SPC Control Chart
19a94ed7-40fa-43b5-9868-a2ce2e2b1cef.lifecycle: Updated continuously as in-process samples are measured; reviewed to detect out-of-control (not necessarily out-of-spec) trends.

209c4879-7b77-46e8-91e2-153eac453a77.name: Nonconformance Record
209c4879-7b77-46e8-91e2-153eac453a77.lifecycle: Opened whenever a result fails specification; feeds OOS disposition and potentially CAPA.

76dd2e5a-20a8-4f19-8fc1-3b78be49fee3.name: CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) Record
76dd2e5a-20a8-4f19-8fc1-3b78be49fee3.lifecycle: Opened from a nonconformance or trend; includes root cause (5 Whys/fishbone), action plan, owner, due date, and effectiveness verification; tracked to closure.

9e388600-1312-452b-8d07-3a0efd5202f9.name: Catch Weight Record
9e388600-1312-452b-8d07-3a0efd5202f9.lifecycle: Captured per unit/case at packaging for variable-weight products; used for both inventory count and weight-based pricing. Structurally follows the Catch-Weight Data Model (see that artifact) rather than the single fixed-quantity field most ERP/WMS systems assume.

2e49adf0-dbf5-4625-bfda-b538e7085483.name: Catch-Weight Data Model
2e49adf0-dbf5-4625-bfda-b538e7085483.lifecycle: Defines how a variable-weight unit is represented so a single fixed quantity field (what most standard ERP/WMS modules assume) doesn't lose information. Every catch-weight unit carries two independent quantities that must both be tracked and both flow through to invoicing: count (1 case, 1 each) and actual weight (e.g. 42.3 lb), plus the unit of measure the weight is captured in. Nominal/catalog weight (the average or list weight used for planning and initial pricing estimates) is stored separately from actual weight (the real, measured value for that specific unit) — the two are expected to diverge and inventory/financial calculations must use actual, not nominal. Variable-measure trade items use a GTIN with a distinct prefix (not scanned at retail POS like a fixed-quantity GTIN), carried with GS1-128 Application Identifier (01) for the GTIN plus AI (310n)/(320n) for net weight in kg/lb — the standard GS1 mechanism for carrying actual weight from the scale through label, shipping, and invoicing without re-keying. A pure count-based ERP/WMS integration built against this data model needs a parallel weight field wherever quantity is used — receiving, inventory, pricing, and shipping — not just at the point of sale.

a7ea6ca7-6b6e-4101-beff-e581956d8a36.name: Package Integrity / Foreign Material Inspection Record
a7ea6ca7-6b6e-4101-beff-e581956d8a36.lifecycle: Generated per inspection event (metal detector, X-ray) at packaging; rejections logged and disposed per procedure.

6425e8df-c318-495b-ae4e-a3a81af541f1.name: Label Copy Draft
6425e8df-c318-495b-ae4e-a3a81af541f1.lifecycle: Drafted from the BOM and marketing input; revised through QA/regulatory review before artwork.

3afd5ba3-988d-462f-836b-b99ec6c661c2.name: Label Compliance Checklist
3afd5ba3-988d-462f-836b-b99ec6c661c2.lifecycle: Completed during allergen/claims verification. Each line item on the checklist corresponds to one rule in the Label Compliance Check Catalog (see that artifact), which is what makes the checklist a candidate for automated tooling rather than manual review.

d1139caf-37bd-4f97-9220-08feba95bdce.name: Label Compliance Check Catalog
d1139caf-37bd-4f97-9220-08feba95bdce.lifecycle: Maintained by regulatory/QA; extended when a new claim type or allergen regulation is adopted. Enumerates the specific, automatable checks behind allergen/claims verification, each expressed as a comparison between two existing data sources rather than a free-text review: (1) Allergen cross-check — every major allergen present in the Material/Product Specification's ingredient list for the formulation must appear in the label's "Contains" statement, and nothing in the Contains statement may be absent from the ingredients (catches both under- and over-declaration); (2) Claim threshold checks — nutrient-content claims are checked against their FDA-defined numeric threshold, using the calculated Nutrition Facts values for the formulation: "good source of [nutrient]" claims (e.g. fiber) under 21 CFR 101.54 (10–19% Daily Value per serving), "low fat" under 21 CFR 101.62 (≤3g total fat per RACC), and "reduced sodium" under 21 CFR 101.61 (≥25% less sodium than a named reference food, with the reference food declared) — 101.13 provides the general nutrient-content-claim framework these specific sections build on; (3) Structure/format checks — allergen statement placement, type size minimums, and required statements (e.g. gluten-free labeling's <20ppm attestation under 21 CFR 101.91) are present per the applicable regulation for the claim(s) made. A failing check blocks label approval and routes back to formulation or label copy rather than to print; a passing run against all applicable rules is what allows the checklist to close without manual re-review of ingredients the system already had structured data for.

68a4cc0a-b5c0-4ba4-91de-332c4c74c684.name: Label Artwork File
68a4cc0a-b5c0-4ba4-91de-332c4c74c684.lifecycle: Produced by design agency from approved label copy; goes through final approval before print; version-controlled to prevent obsolete artwork use.

f6d69f97-18d8-4a0f-b4fb-77c9320edb1d.name: FSIS Label Submission
f6d69f97-18d8-4a0f-b4fb-77c9320edb1d.lifecycle: Submitted to FSIS for meat/poultry products. Since 2013, and further expanded by a 2023 final rule (88 FR 2793), most meat/poultry labels are self-certified under generic label approval — FSIS pre-approval is now required only for temporary approvals, religious exempt product labels, and labels bearing special statements/claims (e.g. natural, certified gluten-free, health claims, negative/ingredient-absence claims not otherwise generically approvable), not as a default step for the great majority of labels.

ffb44422-779f-4bc8-86be-ff6fa6d73d68.name: Lot / Date Code Record
ffb44422-779f-4bc8-86be-ff6fa6d73d68.lifecycle: Generated at lot coding, encoding production date/shift/line; becomes the join key for all downstream traceability records for that lot.

f2777655-3ff5-43b8-9e51-494f0704b2a6.name: Label Application Verification Record
f2777655-3ff5-43b8-9e51-494f0704b2a6.lifecycle: Generated per in-line vision-system check confirming correct label/lot code applied to the correct product.

70dd593f-d5f5-4ef5-8e57-decefe3ba0a5.name: Shelf-Life / Remaining Shelf-Life Record
70dd593f-d5f5-4ef5-8e57-decefe3ba0a5.lifecycle: Calculated from production/expiration dates; checked against customer minimum-remaining-shelf-life requirements at shipment.

551a4e09-160c-4f45-ac51-2000370afcab.name: Shipping Record
551a4e09-160c-4f45-ac51-2000370afcab.lifecycle: Created at outbound shipment; records lot(s), quantity, and destination — Shipping is one of the Critical Tracking Events (CTEs) defined at 21 CFR 1.1340 under FSMA 204. Its required fields are defined by the Carrier Handoff KDE Set (see that artifact), which is what the plant must actually capture at trailer loading to keep the trace record continuous once custody passes to the carrier.

b5e32a3a-afdb-4264-a583-9bfcf21d6999.name: Carrier Handoff KDE Set
b5e32a3a-afdb-4264-a583-9bfcf21d6999.lifecycle: Defines the specific Key Data Elements 21 CFR 1.1340 requires the shipper to record for the Shipping Critical Tracking Event, since this is the plant's last touchpoint before the trace record depends on a party outside its direct control: traceability lot code(s), product description, quantity and unit of measure, ship-to (recipient) location description, ship-from location description, date shipped, traceability lot code source information, and reference document type/number. GS1 identifiers (GTIN for product, GLN for locations) are a permitted implementation choice for expressing these descriptions where the receiver can translate them, not an FDA-mandated format — the rule itself is identifier-format-agnostic. Transporter identity is explicitly NOT a required KDE: §1.1340 excludes the transporter from the recipient-location KDE by definition, and the rule imposes no separate requirement to capture transporter identity. For cold-chain product, trailer temperature at load (see Temperature Record) is still worth capturing alongside as the plant's own food-safety evidence of condition at handoff, but it is a plant record, not an FSMA 204 KDE. Once the required fields are captured and the Shipping Record is created, continuity of the trace record downstream depends on the receiving party's own Receiving CTE — the plant's obligation is fully discharged by capturing its own KDE set correctly, not by tracking what happens after handoff.

9cc2d1ff-a495-474c-bc2d-5615fe7e94bb.name: Traceability Lot Code (TLC) Record
9cc2d1ff-a495-474c-bc2d-5615fe7e94bb.lifecycle: A new TLC is assigned only at initial packing of a raw agricultural commodity (other than seafood), first land-based receiving of a food from a fishing vessel, or transformation, per 21 CFR 1.1320 — it must NOT be reassigned at other CTEs like shipping, and must be maintained and passed forward through every subsequent shipment.

1188e42e-3097-4203-b657-0f88020f34f0.name: EPCIS Event
1188e42e-3097-4203-b657-0f88020f34f0.lifecycle: Emitted at supply-chain touchpoints (what/where/when/why — EPCIS's standard four core dimensions); GS1 US has published explicit guidance mapping EPCIS 2.0 onto FDA's FSMA 204 CTEs/KDEs, making it an industry-promoted, emerging de facto format for sharing traceability data across company boundaries — not itself an FDA-mandated format.
